questions.question: Why is module-level reactive state dangerous in SSR?
options: It increases memory usage per component
The server process shares module-level singletons across all requests, leaking data between users
Module-level state is not serializable to JSON for hydration
Vue's reactivity system does not work outside a browser environment

questions.question: What is Nuxt's built-in solution for per-request isolated state?
options: ref() with a unique key argument
useState() which creates an isolated instance per request
provide() at the server entry level

questions.question: In vanilla Vue SSR (without Nuxt), what pattern prevents cross-request state pollution?
options: Using Object.freeze() on all reactive state
Declaring all state inside computed properties
A factory function that creates fresh state instances per request, provided via app.provide()
Resetting global state in a beforeEach navigation guard
